ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-04-2024 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2024 Continued To Apr-04-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301

PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY JOHN M. FITZPATRICK
Off Calendar Jul-31-2023 Dismissal As To Party

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2023 Continued To Apr-04-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-19-2022 Set For Trial On Sep-06-22 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Oct-04-2022 Set For Order To Show Cause On Apr-06-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Aug-02-2022 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Jul-21-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar May-19-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs Warren Anderson and Sandra Anderson.
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.
Hanna M. Thompson of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant Riley Power, Inc.
Emily Anselmo of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendants General Electric Company and ViacomCBS Inc. NKA Paramount Global.
Janice W. Man of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant Douglass Insulation
Robert D. Bernhardt of TANENBAUM KEALE, LLP (206-889-5081), appearing for defendant Research-Cottrell, Inc. NKA AWT Air Company, Inc.
Bryan L. King of KING & SPALDING (213-443-4347), appearing for defendant Mine Safety Appliances
Company, LLC.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a). Opposition filed by Mine Safety Appliances Company, LLC.

The trial date is September 6, 2022 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is September 16, 2022. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court.

The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 1, 2022. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is August 19, 2022. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 2, 2022. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than June 17, 2022. If Plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar May-19-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than June 2, 2022, plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims as to each defendant. No later than June 2, 2022, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. A case management conference is set for July 21, 2022 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than July 22, 2022. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 5, 2022. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by August 26, 2022 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, September 1, 2022 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
